differential_diagnoses: Nutritional optic neuropathy (B12, folate, or copper deficiency)
next_steps: Obtain serum vitamin A, retinol-binding protein, and vitamin E levels
Obtain serum B12, folate, copper, and zinc levels
Consult pediatric ophthalmology for formal dilated fundus exam and electroretinogram (ERG)
Consult pediatric nutrition for dietary assessment and management of avoidant/restrictive food intake disorder (ARFID)
Complete blood count and peripheral smear to evaluate for markers of nutritional deficiency

differential_diagnoses: Combined nutritional deficiency syndrome (A, C, D, and B-complex)
Nutritional optic neuropathy (e.g., B12 or copper deficiency)
next_steps: Obtain serum levels for Vitamin A (retinol), Vitamin C, Vitamin D, Vitamin E, B12, folate, copper, and zinc
Perform a complete blood count and peripheral blood smear to evaluate for anemia and hematologic markers of deficiency
Consult pediatric ophthalmology for formal electroretinogram (ERG) to assess retinal function
Initiate appropriately dosed vitamin supplementation, starting with Vitamin A and Vitamin C
Consult pediatric nutrition for management of avoidant/restrictive food intake disorder (ARFID)
Measure serum calcium, phosphorus, and alkaline phosphatase to evaluate bone mineral metabolism
Obtain baseline dermatological and ophthalmological assessment for xerophthalmia staging (Bitot spots)
